seo.title: Sora 2 Text & Image to Video — Native Audio + Examples
seo.description: Generate Sora 2 videos from text or images with native voice-over + ambient sound. See examples you can recreate, practical prompt structure, and pay-as-you-go pricing on MaxVideoAI.

hero.title: Sora 2
hero.intro: Cinematic clips from text or a single frame — with native voice + ambient sound.

faqs.q: Is Sora 2 available in Europe / the UK?
faqs.a: Yes. Use Sora 2 from Europe, the UK and most locations where our service is available—no direct OpenAI invite needed.
The current MaxVideoAI Sora 2 route outputs 720p. Use Sora 2 Pro when the selected shot needs 1080p review output.
Yes. Upload a PNG/JPG/WEBP/GIF/AVIF frame (up to ~50 MB) and Sora 2 will animate it based on your motion-focused prompt.

faqs.q: Can I remix or extend existing videos with Sora 2?
faqs.a: This configuration is for text→video and image→video only. Combine multiple clips for longer edits.

faqs.q: How do I keep Sora 2 on-brand?
faqs.a: Use image references from Nano Banana or your own design system, mention brand colors, and keep mood consistent across prompts.

custom.heroDesc2: Describe your scene, choose a duration (4, 8 or 12 seconds), pick 16:9 or 9:16, and let Sora 2 generate polished footage you can use in ads, content or client work.

specSections.title: Prompting & shot language
specSections.intro: This tier responds best to director-style prompts with clear beats and camera intent. Think actions and framing before style adjectives.
items: Lead with subject + action + camera move.
Name lighting and lens mood for consistency.
Keep each clip to one main beat.
Start from a single frame when you need a stable look.

specSections.title: Audio & iteration
specSections.intro: Audio is generated alongside the visuals, so rhythm can be shaped in the same prompt. Use quick variations to lock pacing before scaling.
items: Add one or two key sound cues.
Regenerate small changes to test hooks.

custom.imageTitle: Image-to-Video Workflow with Sora 2 (+ Nano Banana)
custom.imageIntro: One advantage of Sora in MaxVideoAI is pairing it with an image engine like Nano Banana.
imageFlow: Generate a reference frame in Nano Banana that matches your brand style or idea.
Send that still into Sora 2 as Image → Video.
Give Sora a prompt that focuses on motion and timing: how the camera should move, what the subject should do, how the shot should end at 4/8/12 seconds.
Generate, review, tweak just the motion language if needed, regenerate.
imageWhy: Product shots that must stay on-brand
Short looping scenes for ads or UI backgrounds
custom.multishotTitle: Multi-Shot & Sequenced Clips – The Sora 2 “Mini-Film” Trick
custom.multishotIntro1: Sora 2 can interpret multi-step prompts and compress several shots into a single 8 or 12 second clip.
custom.multishotIntro2: With “First… then… finally…” or clear Shot 1/2/3 structure, it stages the clip as a sequence of beats.
multishotTips: Aim for 2–3 shots maximum in one clip.
Give each shot one main action and one clear camera move.
Reuse key elements (same subject, same setting, consistent lighting).
Avoid jumping through five different locations in 8 seconds—keep it focused.
strengths: Film-like camera behavior (dolly, pan, handheld, etc.)
Great for UGC-feeling footage and cinematic inserts
boundaries: The current MaxVideoAI Sora 2 route outputs 720p. Use Sora 2 Pro for 1080p review output.
It’s 4–12 seconds, not long-form. Stitch multiple clips for longer edits.
No video input; start from text or image.
No seeds; iterate by refining the prompt and re-running.
Can struggle with very small or detailed text.
safetyRules: Do not generate real people or public figures (no celebrities, politicians, etc.).
No minors, sexual content, hateful content or graphic violence.
Don’t use another person’s likeness without consent.
Some prompts and input images will be blocked if they violate these principles.
Generic characters and scenes are fine.
Famous people or sensitive prompts may be blocked.
This is how both MaxVideoAI and Sora 2 stay usable and safe for professionals.
custom.comparisonTitle: Sora 2 vs Sora 2 Pro – Quick Overview
comparisonPoints: Sora 2 is your fast 720p idea machine.
Sora 2 Pro is your higher-resolution, more controllable sibling.
Storyboard with Sora 2, then regenerate selected shots in Sora 2 Pro when you need 1080p review output and tighter finishing control.

custom.finalPara1: Sora 2 in MaxVideoAI gives you a direct, pay-as-you-go way to use one of the most impressive short-form video models available – without infrastructure setup or guesswork.
custom.finalPara2: Use it to explore ideas, build mini-sequences, and test creative directions fast. When you need more resolution or control, you can always step up to Sora 2 Pro.

decisionCards.title: Sora 2 or Sora 2 Pro?
decisionCards.body: Use Sora 2 for faster 720p concept passes. Use Pro when the selected shot needs 1080p polish and tighter finishing control.

decisionCards.title: Starting from an image?
decisionCards.body: Upload one clean frame to lock composition, product shape or character direction before writing the motion brief.

decisionCards.title: Comparing premium routes?
decisionCards.body: Compare Sora 2 with Veo 3.1 or Kling 3 Pro when choosing between OpenAI concepts, Google polish and motion-control alternatives.

referenceWorkflows.title: Text prompt
referenceWorkflows.body: Write subject, action, camera, style and one or two sound cues.

referenceWorkflows.title: Image input
referenceWorkflows.body: Use one frame to anchor the opening composition, then prompt only the motion and audio.

referenceWorkflows.title: Duration choice
referenceWorkflows.body: Use 4s for hook tests, 8s for a full beat and 12s for short storyboard sequences.

referenceWorkflows.title: Audio cues
referenceWorkflows.body: Keep dialogue short and tie SFX to visible actions so sound stays useful.

referenceWorkflows.title: Upgrade path
referenceWorkflows.body: Move winning Sora 2 concepts into Sora 2 Pro when you need final polish.

section.title: How to Write a Great Sora 2 Prompt
section.intro: Sora 2 works best when you brief it like a cinematographer: one clear shot, simple timing, and visible actions.
section.tip: Tip: duration + aspect ratio are set in the UI — your prompt controls subject, action, camera, lighting, style, and sound.
section.referencesTitle: How Sora 2 uses prompts, start images and audio

tabs.label: Quick
tabs.title: Quick action prompt
tabs.description: Use this for fast 4s hook tests.
tabs.copy: [Subject] performs [one visible action] in [setting]. Camera: [one move]. Lighting: [time of day / mood]. Audio: [ambience + one SFX].

tabs.label: Structured
tabs.title: Structured video prompt
tabs.description: Use this when the clip needs repeatable action and sound.
tabs.copy:
Subject:
[Who/what + one visible action]

Setting:
[Where + time of day + 2 visual anchors]

Camera:
[Framing + angle + one move]

Lighting:
[Key light + palette + contrast]

Audio:
[Ambience + 1 SFX cue + optional dialogue under 8 words]

Constraints:
No logos. No readable text. No protected likeness.

tabs.label: Pro
tabs.title: Image-to-video prompt
tabs.description: Use this when a single approved still should define the opening frame.
tabs.copy:
Source image role:
[What must stay fixed: subject, product shape, wardrobe or composition]

Motion:
[One action that starts from the still]

Camera:
[Move slowly / push-in / handheld drift / locked-off]

Ending:
[Where the subject or product should land by the final second]

Audio:
[Short ambience + visible SFX]

Avoid:
Changing identity, adding logos, or introducing extra subjects.

tabs.label: Storyboard
tabs.title: Storyboard prompt (sequenced shot list)
tabs.description: Use this when you need multi-beat continuity in a single clip.
tabs.copy:
Storyboard / shot list (same subject + same setting)
Duration: [4/8/12s] • Aspect: [16:9 or 9:16]

Beat 1 (0-2s):
[Framing + subject action + camera move]

Beat 2 (2-6s):
[Action continues, do not change location]

Beat 3 (6-8/12s):
[Final reveal + camera settle]

Continuity:
[Repeat wardrobe, props, lighting, background]

Audio:
[Ambience + 1 SFX cue + short dialogue if needed]

Constraints:
No logos. No readable text. No protected people.

tabNotes.body: Quick = hook tests. Use one action, one camera move and one sound cue.
Structured = consistency. Separate action, camera, lighting, audio and short dialogue.
Pro = controlled delivery. Use continuity anchors and a clear final frame goal.

globalPrinciples: Use visual anchors (specific nouns > vague adjectives)
Start from an image for maximum control
Iterate: change one thing at a time
engineWhy: Too many beats can cause drift — keep each clip to 2–3 clear actions.
Image-to-video locks the first frame composition; prompt controls motion and timing.
Reference image rules are strict — clean stills, no logos, no readable text.
Audio cues help pacing; describe 1–2 key sounds for rhythm.

demo.title: Demo: a sequenced prompt
demo.prompt:
8-second lifestyle spot: a 30-year-old runner with a smartwatch at sunrise in an urban park.
Shot 1 (2s): close-up on the watch as he tightens the strap, sun flare on the glass.
Shot 2 (4s): side tracking shot as he runs, warm light behind buildings.
Shot 3 (2s): close-up on his face glancing at the screen, slight smile, visible breath in cool air.
Lighting: golden hour, natural cinematic tones.
Audio: rhythmic footsteps + soft optimistic music.
Camera: dynamic handheld, 50mm feel.
Negative: no logos, no slow motion, no on-screen text.
notes: Shows transitions, location/subject continuity, clear motion, and a complete mini-story under 10s.
Reuse this structure for skincare, SaaS, fitness, or any 3-beat micro-story.

summary.subject: Urban runner
summary.action: Tightens a smartwatch and accelerates through morning light
summary.camera: Watch close-up, side tracking shot, final face close-up
summary.style: Natural golden hour, premium lifestyle look
summary.output: Rhythmic footsteps, short breath, soft optimistic music
